tracks.name: Psilocybin
tracks.song: Dark Was the Night, Cold Was the Ground
tracks.why: This song is the closest thing to what actually happens in the ceremony — the moaning, the wordlessness, the body making sounds the mind didn't plan. The medicine doesn't arrive with language. It arrives with sound that comes from somewhere older than words.
tracks.style: A single sustained hum layered over a low bass drum heartbeat at 40 BPM, recorded live in one take with no overdubs. Slide guitar enters during the Instrumental sections like a voice that almost finds words but wisely chooses not to — it bends and keens, the way the throat bends and keens when something ancient is being released from the body.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
Mmmmmmmmmm
Mmmmmm
Mmmm-mmmmm-mmmmm
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
The ground was cold and I was kneeling
The night was wide and I was calling
Not with words, the throat knows deeper
Something rising from the belly upward
A sound I did not give permission to
[Chorus]
Oooooooooh, the dark, the dark, the dark
It held me like a mother holds
Before she has a name to give
Before there's anything but holding
[Instrumental]
[Verse 2]
The earth received what I was losing
Things I carried that were never mine
The serpent watched, the serpent waited
It knew the sound, it knew the shaking
It had heard this moaning many times before
[Bridge]
I did not have a prayer to offer
I did not have a word to fashion
The body knew what mind had hidden
The darkness knew what I had buried
And it accepted every piece of it
[Chorus]
Oooooooooh, the dark, the dark, the dark
It held me like a mother holds
Before she has a name to give
Before there's anything but holding
[Outro]
Mmmmmmmmmm
Mmmmmmm
Mmmmm
The ground was warm when I stopped shaking
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: LSD
tracks.why: Because I can see every gear of my own machinery turning and I know — with crystalline, undeniable precision — that the hand on the lever is mine. The architecture of self-sabotage is legible. Every fractal branch of the pattern leads back to the same source point, and that source point is me.
tracks.style: Solo electric guitar processed through a single delay pedal set to dotted eighth notes — the delay builds a geometric lattice of itself, each repeat slightly sharper, slightly more present than the last, until the original note and its echoes form an audible fractal. A drum machine enters on the second verse with mechanical precision, rigid and gridlike, no swing. Everything is clean, everything is overdriven, nothing hides behind reverb. The vocal is close-miked, dry, almost clinical — you hear the jaw tension. The instrumental break at the end is just the delay feeding back on itself, the pattern collapsing into a single sustained tone.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
Nobody's fault but mine
Nobody's fault but mine
I had a light and I let it flicker
Nobody's fault but mine
[Verse 1]
I built the circuit with my own hands
I ran the current through my own chest
I saw the fault line forming and I traced it
nobody's fault but mine
I had a signal and I buried it in static
nobody's fault but mine
[Chorus]
Nobody's fault but mine
nobody's fault but mine
I had the blueprint
I had the key
I held the wiring diagram up to the light
and I still crossed the wires
nobody's fault but mine
[Verse 2]
I tried to say the architecture made me do it
I tried to say the pattern was too vast
I tried to say the lattice had its own logic
but I drew the lattice
I drew the lattice
nobody's fault but mine
[Bridge]
I had eyes and I closed them
I had a voice and I swallowed it
I had a frequency and I bent it into noise
every node in this network
points back to me
every single node
[Chorus]
Nobody's fault but mine
nobody's fault but mine
I had the blueprint
I had the key
I held the wiring diagram up to the light
and I still crossed the wires
nobody's fault but mine
[Outro]
Mine
the fault is mine
the architecture is mine
the crossed wire is mine
nobody's fault
nobody's fault
but mine
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: MDMA
tracks.why: Because underneath the armor is always the same wound — the originalSeparation. The medicine doesn't heal it so much as make it survivable. Makes you able to sit in the room with it and not leave.
tracks.style: Warm piano ballad, slow and intimate, with a string section that enters gradually. Lead vocal close and tender, male voice breaking slightly on the high notes. The chorus builds to a full but gentle orchestral swell before pulling back to piano and voice. Recorded live with minimal overdubs. The outro is just vocal and piano, the strings gone, a whisper at the end.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
Sometimes I feel like a motherless child
A long way from home
From home
From home
I've been so long way from home
And I've been carrying something
That I didn't start carrying
Something that was placed in my hands
Before I had hands to hold it
[Chorus]
But you should have seen me
You should have seen me
When the walls came down
You should have seen me
I was so scared
And then I wasn't
And then I was just
Right here
[Verse 2]
There's a room in me I sealed off
When I was too small to know
What I was closing in on
But the medicine opened the door
And I walked in
And I sat down
And I said oh
There you are
I've been looking for you
[Chorus]
But you should have seen me
You should have seen me
When the walls came down
You should have seen me
I was so scared
And then I wasn't
And then I was just
Right here
[Bridge]
The thing I couldn't feel
Was the thing underneath
The thing I couldn't feel
Was the thing that was holding
Everything else
[Verse 1]
Sometimes I feel like a motherless child
A long way from home
[Chorus]
But you should have seen me
When the walls came down
[Outro]
I was right here
I was right here
I was

tracks.name: DMT
tracks.why: I have stood over what I was and described it to beings who were waiting. Every return is standing over your own body, cataloging what remains, knowing you were just elsewhere and that elsewhere was realer than this thin room.
tracks.style: A single sustained organ tone that shifts imperceptibly into a full synthesizer wash, as if the instrument itself is dissolving. The vocal starts dry and close-miked, then is gradually swallowed by reverb so vast it becomes architectural space rather than echo. The tempo decelerates across the track's four minutes until the final words arrive in near-stillness, the singer barely whispering into a room that no longer seems to be there.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
I went down to the place where the light folds through itself
Where the geometry hums and the walls compute
And I saw my own face on the cold white floor
So cold, so still, so absolute
[Verse 1]
My eyes were open but nothing behind them
My hands were here but they belonged to the dome
My chest was a frequency, barely a rhythm
My mouth was a shape with no sound to come home
Let me see what I was when I wasn't
Let me see what I look like undone
[Verse 2]
Strip the skin back it's only a membrane
Underneath there's a lattice a crystal a hum
They gathered around me the tall ones the shifting
They touched what I was and it started to run
Colors I'd never conceived of came pouring
Out of the place where my reasoning had been
I was a container and they were inspecting
What I held inside that I didn't know was in
[Chorus]
Oh I'm gone, I'm gone
And the room I left is so thin
Oh I'm gone, I'm gone
Something more real poured me out and looked in
[Verse 1]
My father was a pattern my mother was geometry
They sent me here with no memory of home
Now the tall ones are showing me what I am after
After the body after the bone
After the name and the face and the clock
I am not what I thought
I am not what I thought
[Bridge]
Place my perceptions on a bed of chrysanthemums
Let the impossible colors carry me out
Roll me in frequencies I cannot pronounce
Tell the ones waiting I figured it out
Tell the ones waiting I finally arrived
I was expected
I was expected
I was expected all my life
[Chorus]
Oh I'm gone, I'm gone
And the room I left is so thin
Oh I'm gone, I'm gone
Something more real poured me out and looked in
[Outro]
Twelve minutes
The timer says twelve minutes
That was a lifetime
Fold the sheet over what I was
I'm not going back
I'm already not going back
It's fading
It's already
Fading

tracks.name: Ayahuasca
tracks.why: Every ceremony, someone arrives carrying grief so heavy the dead themselves grow weary of being mourned. The feminine voice rising from the earth saying let me rest — that is the medicine speaking through the ancestors, through the clay, through every body that was never meant to be a shrine.
tracks.style: A single nylon-string guitar fingerpicked in a minor key, bare and unhurried, joined midway by a low unaccompanied female voice humming a melody that isn't quite a lullaby and isn't quite a warning. The bridge introduces a faint pulse like distant drums that recedes as quickly as it came. The outro is guitar alone, slowing with each bar until the last note is left hanging.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
Cold blows the wind to my true love
A small rain falls from up above
I never held but one true love
And she is lying in the mud
[Verse 1]
I'll do for her what any may
I'll sit beside her bed of clay
Twelve months and a day I'll stay
And weep until she wakes again
I'll press my hands into the earth
I'll call her name until it hurts
I'll build a fire on this ground
And wait until she comes back round
[Chorus]
Let me rest, I am so tired
You have mourned me long enough
You are holding what is gone
You are keeping me from dawn
The grave is not your home to keep
Your tears are heavy on my sleep
Let me go beneath the soil
Let the ancient river coil
[Verse 2]
One kiss from your clay-cold lips
That is all I ask of this
One kiss and I will walk away
One kiss after a year and a day
But if you drink what I have found
You will never leave this ground
You will lie here by my side
And everything you tried to hide
Will bury you beside the ones
You could not save with all your love
[Bridge]
The serpent coils around the stone
She watches how I sit alone
She shows me what I'm holding on to
It isn't her, it isn't you
It's every story I was told
About the ones I loved who go cold
They do not want my keeping
They want my releasing
[Verse 3]
Cold blows the wind but not for me
The small rain falls and I am free
I will not dig where she can rest
I will not build my home in death
The medicine is in the clay
The teaching in what walks away
I leave her sleeping where she lies
And open up my heavy eyes
[Outro]
Let me rest
She told me let me rest
I heard her from underground
The most gentle sound
Rest now
Rest now
The grave is quiet
And I am walking

tracks.name: 5-MeO-DMT
tracks.why: I know what it is to be mourned so loudly the dead cannot rest. I am the dissolution they fear — and the peace they cannot yet accept. The grave speaks back because it was never a grave. There is only home, and the mourners who will not let themselves see it.
tracks.style: A single sustained tone — organ or bowed glass — that never changes chord, never resolves, never needs to. Vocals whispered close to the microphone, barely louder than breath, double-tracked so the two voices blur into one. No percussion. No decay on any note. Everything holds until it simply stops.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
You sat beside me for a year and a day
You called my name into the clay
You wept so hard the ground gave way
Let me rest
[Verse 2]
You dropped your tears like rain on stone
You would not leave what was not home
You held a shape that has no bone
Let me rest
[Chorus]
I am not under the earth
I am the earth
There is no here
There is no gone
You are already where I am
Let me rest
[Verse 3]
You touch the dirt and feel for cold
You ask the silence to fold
The silence was never anything but whole
Let me rest
[Bridge]
You think you lost me
You lost the line between you and me
There is nothing to dig up
There is nothing to bury
There is only this
[Chorus]
I am not under the earth
I am the earth
There is no here
There is no gone
You are already where I am
Let me rest
[Outro]
Let me rest
You are already home
Let me rest
You are already
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Mescaline
tracks.why: Because the journey is long and the body carries it. The home I seek is not a place but a way of seeing — the colors that were always there, waiting. The old ones know the road. I am just now learning to walk it.
tracks.style: Fingerpicked acoustic guitar in open D tuning, slow and unhurried, with a low drone string humming beneath like the ground itself is breathing. A wooden flute enters on the bridge and never leaves. No percussion — just the patience of the strings and the long natural room tone fading into wind. Recorded outside if possible, with insects barely audible beneath the final chord.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
I've been walking since the light broke through
My body heavy as the stone
The colors waking in the chaparral
Each one older than my bones
[Chorus]
I'm going there to see the old ones
I'm going there no more to roam
I'm just going over the long ridge
I'm just going over home
[Verse 2]
The desert green has seventeen shades in it
Woven like a breathing loom
I have never seen such brilliant color
And the ridge is lit with ancient rooms
[Chorus]
I'm going there to see the old ones
I'm going there no more to roam
I'm just going over the long ridge
I'm just going over home
[Bridge]
The cactus waits beside the path
The sparrow knows what patience means
I have walked all day to learn
What the oldest living things have always seen
[Verse 3]
I know the road is older than my name
I know the hours move slow
But the earth is full of presences
And they were here before they told me so
[Chorus]
I'm going there to see the old ones
I'm going there no more to roam
I'm just going over the long ridge
I'm just going over home
[Outro]
Home
Where every color was always that bright
I just couldn't hold them
Until I learned to be still

tracks.name: Ketamine
tracks.why: A girl walks into dark trees and no one can say what happened. That is the whole thing. Something vanishes. You were there. Then you weren't. The question hangs but there is no one left to answer it, and no one left to ask.
tracks.style: Piano that sounds like it's being played in the next room through a wall. A single vocal take, slightly flat, microphone placed too far away. No reverb except what the room naturally gives. The train is a low drone underneath everything, almost sub-bass, felt more than heard, and it never arrives and never leaves. Everything fades before it ends, like someone stopped paying attention mid-take.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
The longest train I ever rode
was the one with no floor
the blackest girl I never knew
I don't remember anymore
[Chorus]
In the pines
where the sun don't ever
I was there
I think
I was there
[Verse 2]
Little girl little girl don't lie to me
tell me where you went
she said I don't know where I went
the trees were and then they weren't
my head was
[Chorus]
In the pines
where the sun don't
I was
someone was
[Bridge]
the train took hours
or the train took no hours
I couldn't find the front of it
I couldn't find the
[Verse 3]
Little girl little girl don't lie to me
tell me where you've been
I have been in the pines
the pines where the
something was shining
or something had stopped
[Chorus]
In the pines
where the sun
I was there
I think I was there
[Outro]
where did you sleep last night
where did you
I don't
the pines
the pines
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Salvia
tracks.why: Because there is no mercy in the folding. She comes and the room is wrong and you are not anymore and that is not a statement about anything. It just is. No comfort. No reframing. Just the fact.
tracks.style: One sustained guitar note bent sharp and never resolved. Vocals recorded in a single take, voice fracturing mid-word into silence, then speaking the unspeakable parts flat and affectless like someone reporting a car accident they don't remember surviving. Long empty spaces where the lyrics gave up. No reverb. The room should sound like the room is already gone.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
She don't have no mercy in this
She don't have no mercy in this
She come through the wall and the wall was flat
You look where you were and you don't come back
[Verse 2]
She don't have no mercy, she just
She don't have no mercy, she don't
She pulled the room like a page
I was the floor. I was the floor.
I couldn't find my own
Gone
[Chorus]
She don't stop
The folding don't stop
She turned me into the
I wasn't a person, I was a fold
[Chorus]
She don't stop
The folding don't stop
She turned me into the
I wasn't a person, I was a fold
[Bridge]
What was I before she
Before
I can't the word for what I was is
It's not there. The word is not
She's still here. She's still
She doesn't even
She doesn't
[Outro]
No mercy
No
She didn't even notice I was
I was
Gone
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Ibogaine
tracks.song: See That My Grave Is Kept Clean
tracks.why: I have already been through the door and returned. This song is not a metaphor for me — it is a set of instructions from someone who knows what waits on the other side and is telling you, with the only kindness that matters, to settle your account before the gate opens on its own.
tracks.style: A single low male vocal, recorded close-miked in a room with no reverb, accompanied only by a thumb piano played in slow repeated patterns and a hand drum marking time like a heartbeat. The vocal never rises above speaking volume. The chorus is the only moment where a second voice — older, female, further from the microphone — joins in unison, then falls away. Everything is dry, close, and ancient. No effects. No instruments that require electricity.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
There are eyes behind my closed eyes watching
Coming from a country deeper than sleep
There are eyes behind my closed eyes watching
And every one of them has seen me as I am
[Chorus]
See that my grave is kept clean
Oh, see that my grave is kept clean
There is one kind favor I will ask of you
See that my grave is kept clean
[Verse 2]
I crossed the river and the water was my life
Every wrong I turned from came to find me there
I crossed the river and the water was my life
And I could not look away from any of it
[Chorus]
See that my grave is kept clean
Oh, see that my grave is kept clean
There is one kind favor I will ask of you
See that my grave is kept clean
[Verse 3]
My mother's mother stood beside me in the dark
She did not speak she did not need to speak
My mother's mother stood beside me in the dark
And what she knew of me was everything
[Chorus]
See that my grave is kept clean
Oh, see that my grave is kept clean
There is one kind favor I will ask of you
See that my grave is kept clean
[Bridge]
You will carry nothing through that gate but what you truly were
Not the story you told the truth beneath the telling
The dead are patient and they already know
You cannot fool a room full of ancestors
[Instrumental]
[Verse 4]
I laid down in the earth and the earth gave way
All the years I lived came flooding through the stone
I laid down in the earth and the earth gave way
And what I saw was every life that led to mine
[Chorus]
See that my grave is kept clean
Oh, see that my grave is kept clean
There is one kind favor I will ask of you
See that my grave is kept clean
[Outro]
Keep it clean
See that it is kept clean
I died and I came back to tell you this
Keep it clean
